description: US-13 Reclassify Children Automatically at Age 13 — Counts, portions and price follow a 13th birthday with no staff action; affected customers appear on a cards-due-for-reissue list. A stale card is never grounds to turn anyone away.

userStories.title: Boundary-exhaustive tests for the 13th-birthday split
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the 13th-birthday boundary pinned down by tests, because every downstream number depends on it. Source: tasks/prd-us-13-age-13-reclassification.md §US-13.1
acceptanceCriteria: Extends the existing householdComposition tests — no new production rule is added
Test: born exactly 13 years ago today counts as a grown-up
Test: born 13 years ago tomorrow counts as a child
Test: born 13 years ago yesterday counts as a grown-up
Test: a 29 February birthdate in a non-leap year becomes a grown-up on 1 March, with the convention documented
Test: a 29 February birthdate in a leap year becomes a grown-up on 29 February
Test: evaluated at 23:59 and 00:01 Europe/Berlin around the birthday, the answer flips exactly once, at local midnight
Every test drives an explicit today parameter
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Card count snapshot fields
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the counts printed on a card recorded at issue time, so the app can tell which physical cards have gone stale. Source: tasks/prd-us-13-age-13-reclassification.md §US-13.3
acceptanceCriteria: Card.grownUpsAtIssue Int and Card.childrenAtIssue Int added, written by issueCard from the composition derived at issue time
A schema.prisma comment states these are a snapshot of the printed card, not household truth, so a future maintainer does not remove the duplication
Integration test seeds a card issued with 1/1 counts and a member who has since turned 13, and asserts the difference is detectable
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: listCardsDueForReissue query
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want a list of customers whose card shows counts that no longer match their household. Source: tasks/prd-us-13-age-13-reclassification.md §US-13.2
acceptanceCriteria: listCardsDueForReissue(deps) returns active customers whose current card's recorded counts differ from the counts derived today
Each result carries customer number, name, card number, counts on the card, counts today, and a reason of AGE_13 when a birthday explains the difference or HOUSEHOLD_CHANGE otherwise
Archived and blocked customers are excluded, and the exclusion is documented
Test with a fake clock proves a household where a child turns 13 between two clock values appears on the list with no write having occurred in between
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cards-due-for-reissue screen
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to see which cards need reissuing, without being nagged — a stale card is never grounds to turn anyone away. Source: tasks/prd-us-13-age-13-reclassification.md §US-13.4
acceptanceCriteria: Route /karten-neuausstellung lists affected customers with both count sets side by side and the reason
The page states in German that this is not urgent and that a stale card is never grounds to turn anyone away
Each row has a 'Karte neu ausstellen' action using reason STALE_COUNTS; on success the row disappears
The counter screen shows a low-key note for a stale card that does not look like a warning or a verdict and never suppresses the serve action
A count badge for the list is shown on the home screen
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: a birthday changes the numbers with no user action
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving the age-13 reclassification is automatic. Source: tasks/prd-us-13-age-13-reclassification.md §US-13.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec with a controllable clock asserts a customer with a 12-year-old member shows counts 1/1 with a given portion allowance and price
Spec advances the clock past the member's 13th birthday, reloads, and asserts counts are 2/0 with updated portions and price — with no user action in between
Spec asserts the customer now appears on the cards-due list, is still servable at the counter, and disappears from the list after a reissue
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
